title: Length of Linked List
description: Given values representing a singly linked list, build the list and print its length.
inputFormat: n space-separated integers representing the nodes
outputFormat: Print the number of nodes in the list
intendedApproachSummary: Build the list, then traverse from head to null while counting nodes.
wrongApproachHints: Do not just print n from input if the exercise is about traversal.
A null head should return length 0.
hints: Start count at 0.
Move the current pointer each step.

examples.explanation: There are five nodes in the list.

title: Search in Linked List
description: Given a linked list and a target value x, print Found if x exists in the list, otherwise Not Found.
edgeCases: Target at the head or tail
intendedApproachSummary: Walk node by node until the value is found or the pointer becomes null.
wrongApproachHints: Do not stop after checking only the head.
Compare node data, not node references.
hints: Return early once a match is found.
Otherwise continue to curr.next.

title: Insert at Head
description: Insert a new value x at the head of the linked list and print the updated list.
outputFormat: Print the updated list as space-separated integers
intendedApproachSummary: Create a new node, point it to the current head, then update head to the new node.
wrongApproachHints: Do not lose the old head before linking it.
Empty lists still need the new node as head.
hints: Set newNode.next first.
Then move head to newNode.

examples.explanation: The new value becomes the first node.

title: Insert at Tail
description: Insert a new value x at the end of the linked list and print the updated list.
intendedApproachSummary: Traverse to the last node, then attach the new node. If the list is empty, the new node becomes head.
wrongApproachHints: Do not stop one node too early.
Handle the empty-list case separately.
hints: Keep moving until curr.next is null.
Then assign curr.next = newNode.

examples.explanation: 8 is appended after the last node.

title: Delete at Position
description: Delete the node at 1-based position p from the linked list and print the updated list.
intendedApproachSummary: If p is 1, move head forward. Otherwise stop at the node before p and reconnect its next pointer.
wrongApproachHints: Be careful with 1-based indexing.
You need the previous node before the node to delete.
hints: Walk to position p - 1.
Skip the next node by rewiring pointers.

examples.explanation: The value 1 at position 3 is removed.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Reverse a singly linked list and print the reversed list.
intendedApproachSummary: Use prev, curr, and next references to reverse every next pointer in one pass.
wrongApproachHints: If you do not store next first, you can lose the rest of the list.
Return the new head, which is prev at the end.
hints: Save curr.next before changing it.
Advance prev and curr after rewiring.

title: Middle of Linked List
description: Print the value of the middle node of the linked list. For even length, print the second middle.
intendedApproachSummary: Use slow and fast pointers. Move slow by one step and fast by two steps until fast reaches the end.
wrongApproachHints: A two-pass length solution works, but this problem is meant for fast-slow pointers.
Check both fast and fast.next before jumping two steps.
hints: Start both pointers at head.
When fast stops, slow is at the middle.

examples.explanation: 3 is the middle of the list.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given a linked list and a position pos, connect the tail to the node at 1-based position pos if pos > 0. Print Cycle if the resulting list has a cycle, otherwise No Cycle.
intendedApproachSummary: Construct the optional cycle, then use Floyd's cycle detection with fast and slow pointers.
wrongApproachHints: Comparing node values is not enough; compare node references.
Stop if fast or fast.next becomes null.
hints: Move slow by one and fast by two.
If they meet, a cycle exists.

examples.explanation: The tail connects back to the second node.

title: Merge Two Sorted Linked Lists
description: Given two sorted linked lists, merge them into one sorted linked list and print the result.
intendedApproachSummary: Use a dummy head and attach the smaller front node each step, then append the remaining list.
wrongApproachHints: Do not lose the dummy head reference.
Remember to append leftovers after one list ends.
hints: Maintain a tail pointer.
Compare current nodes from both lists.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Linked List
description: Given a sorted linked list, remove duplicate nodes so each value appears once, then print the cleaned list.
intendedApproachSummary: Walk the list and when two neighboring nodes have the same value, skip the duplicate node.
wrongApproachHints: This only works directly because the list is sorted.
After deleting a duplicate, keep checking the same current node.
hints: Compare curr.data with curr.next.data.
Only advance curr when the next node is different.
